title: Angianthus strictus
taxonomy: Angianthus strictus is classified within the family Asteraceae, which includes a large number of flowering plant species commonly known as sunflowers or daisies. This family is further divided into numerous subfamilies and tribes. Within the Asteraceae family, Angianthus strictus is typically placed in the subfamily Asteroideae, which contains many plants native to Australia and other parts of the world. The genus Angianthus is a group of shrub-like plants native to Western Australia, and Angianthus strictus is one of several species within this genus. Further classification of Angianthus strictus can also involve its tribe and section within the genus, although this may vary depending on the source.

intro: Angianthus strictus, also known as the Blue Button or Button Daisy, is a shrub-like plant native to Western Australia, belonging to the Asteraceae family. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, potentially providing relief from conditions such as arthritis and reducing oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Angianthus strictus include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to support overall health and well-being.
